author: @marks.ebikepacking.adventures
text: Always good to watch - even two years after their first airing. I love this kind of thing. With a piece of land you set up a group of these things daily clocking up milliamps to trickle feed a battery system. I think to feel we're going to get the kind of feed we get from the mains is fanciful but a trickle feed is enough with other system like solar in the energy converting loop.

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: I would avoid cutting the drum and I would probably stick (rivetted) some angle shaped thin metal  or plastic on a outer surface, Maybe at the angle in relation to the axes of rotation , to maximise surface  in contact with the wind. Seems that you are more focussed on electrical (generating) bit of the wind generator , than you try to adress aerodynamic side. However , I get that simplicity is the key and you work with what you have. Also , I can't wait to see some ideas about charging controller for a windmill (DIY style , of course). I quite like the your videos from last couple months , keep it that way , if possible. All the best !

author: @kreynolds1123
text: Small vawt may get away with nothing but radial bearings but the bottom bearing of a medium to large VAWT may have relativly heavy or large radial loads in heavy winds and thrust loads from its weight. Taper bearing would work best for top and bottom, especially if the top is secured by a cross held in position with guide wires.

author: @kiddiescripterkiller
text: After the first time slicing my hand open over 40 years ago, I always wear gloves when playing with sheet metal...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: in over 50 years I have never cut my hand on sheet metal - it's just how you handle it - I mean have you ever cut a tomato with gloves on? Of course not you handle the knife properly and there is no danger - if you don't handle it properly then yeah wear gloves

replies.author: @hanslepoeter5167
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering it's more the kind of gloves you use handeling the grinder. You might be many kids technological hero and set the example 😃

replies.author: @kiddiescripterkiller
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering True you have to be careful, but things happen... Sometimes it is so sharp you don't even know you got cut until you try and figure out where the hell all the blood is coming from.

Sliced my knuckle open when I picked up a screw driver off a piece of sheet metal from a radial arm saw I was working on. The edge was so sharp, I didn't even feel it... And I don't remember grazing my knuckle against the edge sticking up, I just grabbed the screw driver and picked it up. I only noticed I sliced my knuckle open, when I dropped a screw on the floor and went to pick it up and saw blood on the shop floor, and thought... WTF... Then I noticed it on the bench and the saw I was working on... I was bleeding like a stuck pig. I had blood all over my hand and running down my arm. And after figuring out and finally seeing where it was coming from, then it started hurting... ;) really bad... lol

I should have went to the hospital, it was that deep. But I hate going to the doctors... So I straitened my finger so the slice would stay closed, wiped the blood off and quickly wrapped it in electrical tape. Got nervous when it swelled up with blood under the skin, but it was fine after a few days...

After that was when I decided to wear gloves while playing with or near sheet metal...

replies.author: @leorigby3069
replies.text: In my college days I walked past someone's freshly cut steel gear tray they had left in a vice and it sliced through my overalls and into my arm. Didn't even feel it, just felt a growing wet patch!

author: @Mr.Unacceptable
text: A word about bearings. Skate bearings are made to take stress though the race and outer ring. Not sideways like this. This will fail early because the bearings will be forced to run on the edge of the groove. If you use a cone bearing. You can get one for the proper weight of the item it's meant to hold. If the bearing needs 10F lb's to load it. Then a 10lb object will do the same thing. Meaning all those ball bearings will be running in the track they are meant to run under the least stress. Weight your drum and get a rear axle cone bearing that is the closest torque rating as the weight of the drum. Also, the size you want. I'll bet a Toyota or Hyundai small car rear axel will have what you need. They also come in almost any size and load rating and found at any junkyard or parts shop.

replies.author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
replies.text: Called tapered bearings. Front wheel spindles.  Take the grease out and they have little friction. That drum weighs nothing compared to the load of 1/4 of the car.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text: Yeah, the bearing used in the video are designed for radial loads,  not for thrust loads. Taper bearings are better when one has both thrust and radial loads.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text: @MAGnetICus_Attractus Bicycle cup and cone bearings might reasonably deal with the thrust and radial loads that this drum will exert.

replies.author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
replies.text: @kreynolds1123 I also figure when one side wears out I will just flip the wheel over and wear out the other set.

replies.author: @Mr.Unacceptable
replies.text: You could also use a centre post, weld on a thick washer and use a flat roller bearing. Thrust bearing?  Then you could use the skate board bearing to keep it centred.  I still think the cone bearing is the better option.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
A few of those bearings mounted vertically on rods or bolts welded in a star pattern will help take the weight on the bottom.
A great way of making a large squirrel cage, could have used two shifting spanners to help you bend the fins out to the right angle.
With the motor, I have seen grooves cut out of the metal ring housing the coils and gluing in magnets in a N, S, N, S configuration before putting the housing back on to boost the motor (come generator) performance.
I got an awesome 3 phase motor from a front loading washing machine that is a ring of coils sitting in a cast dish with N, S, N, S rare earth magnets. It is only good for a windmill type wind generator after being converted to remove the 3rd phase or using circuitry to convert the 3 phase to regular AC. Still an awesome motor for free.
